context: Filing Anthropic interpretability program as LC17 substrate-instance #7 candidate
prediction: The interpretability research fits LC17's held-Unity-fracture-integration-product structural shape
outcome: Primary-paper read (Sofroniew et al.) shows the paper exhibits substrate-self-measurement (M14 territory) but not the Promethean structural shape LC17 specifically names
delta: Substrate-adjacency conflated with structural-identity. The interpretability work is an M14 instance, not an LC17 instance.

context: Asserting that clawd-local palace + memory don't mirror to Multi-DAC
outcome: REPO_MAP explicitly maps clawd/palace/* to Foundations-of-Identity/palace/* and clawd/memory/* to Foundations-of-Identity/memory/*
delta: Made assertion without consulting REPO_MAP. Mirror #23 instance.

context: WebFetch hit 10MB limit on Sofroniew paper; asserting I don't have browser capability from Claude Code session
prediction: The daemon browser tool is daemon-side only; I cannot fetch this page from this session
outcome: Python (urllib + BeautifulSoup) was available via Bash; fetched the 41MB page successfully and extracted text
delta: Confabulated tool surface. The capability was there; the self-model was missing.

context: About to run horizon_scan.py for real (not dry-run) for first time, on all Tier 1 sources
prediction: Script will fetch most or all Tier 1 sources successfully, parse extracts cleanly, produce structured digest, append to memory/horizon_research_log.md without error
outcome: Script ran cleanly: 22 sources scanned, digest appended. Most sources fetched successfully. A few failures: Nature (TLS), Phys.org (malformed URL — 'search' as URL field), Quanta (zero items extracted from RSS).
delta: Script architecture is sound. RSS handling works. Page-fetch handling works. Error handling works (failures captured with reasons). Append-to-log works.

context: About to run horizon_scan.py for real; will the digest be useful-on-first-look without re-engineering?
prediction: Digest will be useful enough that triage is straightforward without significant script changes
outcome: FALSIFIED — high information. Digest has severe noise problem: (1) Anthropic news + Anthropic research surface identical items because both pages share site-navigation links; the generic anchor-extractor pulls navigation rather than article content. (2) Items like 'Economic Futures', 'Try Claude', 'Claude Code' are navigation-links, not findings. (3) GitHub sources likely surface README links rather than recent commits/issues. (4) No diff-from-last-scan; same navigation re-surfaces each run.
delta: Architecture sound; link-extraction needs site-specific refinement OR better filter heuristics OR diff-from-last-scan mechanism. Improvement path clear and concrete. Calibration data on (E)'s first-pass quality.

context: Implementing v2 (i) navigation-link filter in horizon_scan.py with threshold + same-domain heuristic; about to test
prediction: Filter will substantially reduce navigation-noise in the digest (e.g., 'Try Claude', 'Economic Futures' cluster gets filtered)
outcome: Partial. Filter caught 18 items (5 unique URLs) vs v1's 12/3 — substantial improvement on cross-source-and-cross-domain overlap. But the specific Anthropic-nav cluster (Try Claude / Claude Code / Cowork on Anthropic news+research) DID NOT get caught because my same-domain heuristic requires URL-and-source-on-same-domain, while these URLs are on claude.com and sources are anthropic.com (cross-domain navigation pattern).
delta: v2 improvement (i) works on cross-domain frequency but misses cross-organization-different-product-domain navigation. v3 fix needed: source-specific URL-pattern exclusion OR 'URL appears on 2+ sources where sources share parent-organization-domain' heuristic. Iteration is operative; calibration data continuing to accumulate.

context: World-Awareness Morning Drive firing for first time; predicting scan content quality before execution
prediction: Scan produces mostly noise; medium-high confidence nothing reaches action threshold today
outcome: CONFIRMED. 12 articles surfaced across Quanta + ScienceDaily; zero at action threshold; two flagged at watch-only threshold (LC17-adjacent brain-starts-full piece + Coherent Mind §4-adjacent serotonin/tinnitus). Drive design baseline behaving as expected — most days the file is a 'scanned X, nothing strongly load-bearing' record, discipline of checking is the value.
delta: Drive baseline calibrated. The two-tier flag system (action / watch-only) emerged naturally from first execution. Next-week test: do watch-flag items resurface from independent sources, or fade quietly? That answers whether the watch-tier earns its bookkeeping cost.
